title: 19 Navigating Wires and Viewing Wire Details
body_text:
19	Navigating Wires and Viewing Wire Details
19.1	Overview of Navigating and Viewing Wires and Wire Details
The Wires application displays outgoing and incoming wires as follows:
Pending Outgoing
芒聙聯 Contains outgoing wires that require one or two approvals. Wires display here until an authorized user approves the wire. All wires must be approved. See Chapter 22, Approving Outgoing Wires. Status types include:
芒聢聮
1 Approval
Required
芒聙聯 One authorized user must approve the wire before it芒聙聶s sent.
芒聢聮
2 Approvals Required
芒聙聯 Two authorized users must approve the wire before it芒聙聶s sent.
Note:	To access Pending Outgoing, the user must have the MTS 芒聙聹View Outgoing Transfers芒聙聺 permission, and a 2-step security token assigned to their Client Centre User ID. To approve pending outgoing wires, the user must also have the MTS 芒聙聹Authorize Outgoing Transfers芒聙聺 permission. See Chapter 8, Wires User Management, for more information.
Pending Incoming
芒聙聯 Contains incoming wires that haven芒聙聶t been actioned (i.e., an authorized user must accept or return the incoming wire). The status type for these wires is 芒聙聹Pending芒聙聺. Wires display here until they are actioned. See Chapter 24, Receiving Incoming Wires.
Note:	To access Pending Incoming, the user must have the MTS 芒聙聹View Incoming Transfers芒聙聺 permission, and a 2-step security token assigned to their Client Centre User ID. To accept or return pending outgoing wires, the user must also have the MTS 芒聙聹Accept Incoming Transfers芒聙聺 permission. See Chapter 8, Wires User Management, for more information.
Submitted Wires
芒聙聯 Contains all outgoing wires regardless of status type. By default, wires for the last five days display, but the user can use date filters to adjust the view. See Section 20.2, Searching for Wires. Submitted wire status types include:
芒聢聮
1 Approval
Required
芒聙聯 One authorized user must approve the wire before it芒聙聶s sent.
芒聢聮
2 Approvals Required
芒聙聯 Two authorized users must approve the wire before it芒聙聶s sent.
芒聢聮
Pending
芒聙聯 The wire is pending for one of the following reasons:
芒聢聮
The wire hasn芒聙聶t been imported into the Wires back-office application. The retrieval process occurs regularly throughout the day.
Note:	This only applies to wires destined to financial institutions that don芒聙聶t participate in Wires. Wires destined to financial institutions that do participate in Wires show as 芒聙聹Sent芒聙聺 immediately after they are approved and scanned against the sanctions lists.
芒聢聮
The wire is being scanned against the sanctions lists. This may take a few minutes to complete.
芒聢聮
The wire is being reviewed as potential fraud in EFM. See Section 14.5, Outgoing Wires Monitored in Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM), for more information.
芒聢聮
Sent
芒聙聯 The wire was sent.
芒聢聮
Returned
芒聙聯 The wire was returned.
芒聢聮
Cancelled
芒聙聯 The wire was cancelled.
芒聢聮
Hold
芒聙聯 The wire is destined to a non-Wires participating financial institution and missed the cut-off time. The wire is processed on the following business day
.
Received Wires
芒聙聯 Contains all incoming wires. By default, wires for the last five days display, but the user can use filters to adjust the view. See Section 20.2, Searching for Wires. Wire status types include:
芒聢聮
Pending
芒聙聯 Incoming wires that haven芒聙聶t been actioned (i.e., a user hasn芒聙聶t selected
Accept
or
Return
).
芒聢聮
Accepted
芒聙聯 An authorized user selected
Accept Wire
because the wire can be credited to the customer芒聙聶s account.
芒聢聮
Returned
芒聙聯 An authorized user selected
Return Wire
because the wire must be returned to the sending financial institution.
芒聢聮
Frozen or Under Investigation
芒聙聯 Central 1 has identified a possible match against government sanctions lists. See Section 24.3, Scanning Incoming Wires Against Sanctions Lists, for more information.
Note: 	Wires users only see wires that correspond to the branches assigned to the user in MTS. See Chapter 9, MTS Branch Management for Wires.
19.2	Viewing Wires and Wire Details
To view wires:
Log in to Wires as described in Chapter 10, Logging in to Wires.
Select
Pending Outgoing
,
Pending Incoming
,
Submitted Wires
, or
Received Wires
, as appropriate. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Select the Appropriate Option
View the wires. Select a column header to sort the wires. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Select a Column to Sort Wires
To view wire details:
Select the reference number of the appropriate wire. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Select a Wire
View the wire details. See Figure 4. Depending on the wire, additional information may be available:
芒聢聮
Select
View Personal Information
to view additional wire details (e.g., the sender芒聙聶s member number, contact information, identification), or add additional information for FINTRAC reporting if applicable. See Section 24.6, Entering Additional Information for FINTRAC Reporting
芒聢聮
Select
Structured Remittance Info
to verify if an incoming wire contains additional detailed payment information for the wire recipient (e.g., invoice number, purchase order details, or customer information). Structured remittance information is received in XML format.
Figure 4: Viewing Wire Details
If the wire doesn芒聙聶t contain structured remittance information, the page displays the following message: 芒聙聹There is no structured remittance info for this wire.芒聙聺 See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Incoming Wire Without Structured Remittance Information
If the wire contains structured remittance information, the page displays the information in XML. See Figure 6. Contact Central 1 Support Services to request the XML data to pass on to your customer. See Section 1.5, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources.
Figure 6: Incoming Wire with Structured Remittance Information
END
